briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 27, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Change: Increasing Fatal Threats to Human Health and Ecosystems
sections.content: Recent research findings, both domestic and international, warn that the threat of climate change to human health and ecosystems is intensifying. Specifically, high-temperature environments are increasing the antibiotic resistance of Salmonella bacteria, accelerating the spread of superbugs [2, 3, 4, 90, 98, 100, 101, 109, 110, 112, 115], which is identified as a global problem. Furthermore, climate change is reorganizing the habitats of venomous snakes, moving them to new risk areas [1, 103], and raising the possibility of diseases like Chikungunya spreading to temperate regions [96, 107]. In terms of ecosystems, climate change is weakening the natural purification capacity of lakes [11, 95], and directly threatening food supply, with peach farms in Colorado and wild blueberry farms in Maine suffering damage due to disrupted cultivation seasons [93, 94]. Moreover, there is a grim outlook that 10,000 plant species could face extinction by 2100 due to habitat loss [8].

sections.title: ⚠️ Global Climate Crisis Spreading, Posing Serious Challenges to Daily Life and Economy
sections.content: Climate change is having widespread impacts on daily infrastructure and national economies. In the summer of 2026, difficulties for daily infrastructure are projected amidst the climate crisis [13], and Daejeon City is proactively responding to summer natural disasters amid warnings of a super El Niño [15]. Internationally, record-breaking spring heatwaves have occurred across the UK and Western Europe [91, 102, 114, 116], causing suffering to residents, with extreme humidity identified as one of the most overlooked threats [97, 105]. Climate change could lead to shifts in the global geopolitical order [7], and is analyzed to directly impact national economies, with Nigeria's BOI warning that climate change threatens Nigeria's industrial growth [111]. Therapists warn that climate change entails physical and mental risks [92], and in the agricultural sector, the urgency of establishing climate crisis response systems is emphasized [6].

sections.title: 🌱 Continued Efforts Towards a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Amidst this climate crisis, various domestic and international efforts towards a carbon-neutral society are ongoing. K-water has partnered with OpenAI to respond to climate change and disasters [5, 10, 14], and Hannan aims to lead carbon neutrality through its district energy carbon-free working group [17]. In particular, Gwacheon City is encouraging citizen participation by providing over 23 million won in carbon-neutral incentives to households practicing energy saving [19, 20, 21, 29, 30, 38, 46, 52, 53, 56]. Various local governments, including Seongnam City and Geoje City, are also expanding carbon-neutral practices through senior club activities and collaborations with businesses [26, 31, 32, 39, 40, 41, 45, 48, 49]. Furthermore, the biogas industry is emerging as a hidden energy source in the era of carbon neutrality [33], and the Korea Venture Business Association is moving to select and foster excellent venture companies in the carbon-neutral sector [37, 43]. These efforts can be interpreted as positive signals towards achieving carbon neutrality goals.

sections.title: 🌱 Energy Transition Through Renewable Energy Expansion and Technological Innovation
sections.content: In the energy sector, the transition to renewable energy is accelerating, with active policy support and technological innovation. KOSPO is promoting the expansion of pumped-hydro storage and reorganizing its renewable energy division to expand offshore wind and BESS projects [59, 61]. Bonghwa County is emerging as an energy-independent city, recording the highest renewable energy penetration rate nationwide [62, 79, 83]. The Ministry of SMEs and Startups is collaborating with the Gwangju and Jeonnam regions to foster a DC-based renewable energy innovation ecosystem and discussing integration with AI [60, 64, 66, 67, 68, 71, 81, 84, 88], exploring energy transitions that consider regional characteristics. Singapore's Climate Ambassador emphasized that it is the right time for renewable energy investment, citing increasing demand in Korea and the Philippines [70]. Policy support is also strengthening, with the Ministry of Climate stating a preferential policy for applications for renewable energy-based distributed special zones [87]. However, the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) urges a doubling of investment in grids and ESS [72], and there are still criticisms that air pollution from coal use hinders the green transition [86].

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, climate change poses a severe threat to human life and ecosystems worldwide. At the same time, innovative efforts and collaborations to overcome these challenges are actively underway globally. We must recognize both these challenges and opportunities simultaneously and continue our sustainable steps towards the future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
